title: festuca pilgeri subsp. supina
intro: Festuca pilgeri subsp. supina, also known as Supine Fescue, is a type of grass in the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpenes and phenolic compounds. The health benefits associated with Supine Fescue include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and also used as a tincture or in herbal capsules, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
